narrative: OMX30 faces conflicting signals this morning. Technical structure remains constructive—7.8% above 200-MA with MACD bullish and ADX moderately bullish—but correlation headwinds are severe (-0.827% weighted signal) following broad European declines and sharp Asia selloff led by Nikkei's -4.15% plunge. Stochastic overbought conditions (81.7) combined with VIX's +4.50% spike and oil's -3.32% drop suggest profit-taking and demand destruction concerns are overriding domestic bull signals. USD/SEK weakness (-0.41%) hurts major exporters. Risk/reward tilts negative into the Friday close despite technicals remaining above key moving averages.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens under pressure from coordinated European weakness and Asia capitulation. DAX's -73bp decline and Shanghai's -226bp plunge set a risk-off tone despite OMX30's bullish technical posture (+7.8% vs 200-MA, MACD above signal). Stochastic overbought (K/D ~81) signals mean reversion is likely; watch 3085 (lower Bollinger) as first support and 3123 (yesterday close) as pivot. USD/SEK's -41bp drop and oil's -332bp crash pressures Volvo, Atlas Copco, and Ericsson despite sector tailwinds. Defensive rotation into AstraZeneca and Essity warranted. Copper +0.66% offers Boliden a small bright spot. Trade cautiously; Friday liquidity + overbought technicals + negative correlation = elevated whipsaw risk. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET could reignite volatility.
drivers: European weakness (DAX -0.73%, CAC -0.37%, FTSE -0.44%) pressures OMX30 correlation
Asia selloff (Nikkei -4.15%, Shanghai -2.26%) signals risk-off sentiment
Stochastic overbought (K=81.7, D=81.1) flags mean reversion risk despite bull regime
Oil -3.32% relieves industrials input cost pressure but signals demand concern
VIX +4.50% to 19.74 suggests creeping risk aversion despite OMX30 technical strength

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil -3.32% eases input costs, but USD/SEK weakness (-0.41%) and risk-off sentiment weigh on export demand outlook.
No US 10Y data available; macro uncertainty from Asia selloff and VIX spike limit NIM expansion tailwinds.
NASDAQ -0.46% and correlation weakness (-0.827%) pressure sentiment; USD/SEK down limits export pricing power.
Defensive rotation into lower-beta names as risk-off sentiment emerges; VIX +4.50% supports safe havens.
Copper +0.66% and gold +0.48% support Boliden; SSAB pressured by industrial demand concerns.
Oil decline eases some pressure, but H&M faces EUR/SEK stability uncertainty and broader risk-off dampers discretionary spending.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.66% and gold +0.48% provide dual tailwind; isolated bright spot in commodity complex.
USD/SEK down -0.41% pressures export competitiveness; industrial cycle concerns from Asia selloff.
Major exporter facing headwinds from USD/SEK weakness and correlation collapse; oil's -3.32% signals capex caution.
Tech/telecom sentiment damaged by NASDAQ -0.46% and broad risk-off; stochastic overbought may trigger profit-taking.
Defensive outperformance play as VIX +4.50% and correlation collapse drive safe-haven rotation.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET is key volatility trigger for Friday close. No major Swedish data releases. Watch for ECB speakers commenting on growth fears given European selloff.
topRisk: Asia contagion deepens (Nikkei -4.15%, Shanghai -2.26%) and sparks liquidity crunch into Friday close; stochastic overbought combined with negative correlation could trigger sharp OMX30 reversal below 3100. Trade wars escalation or earnings recession signals would accelerate.
technicalSummary: RSI 58.8 neutral—no extreme yet but Stochastic K/D ~81 overbought—mean reversion risk high
ADX 18.1 borderline moderate; reduce confidence by ~10% per rule (ADX not below 18 but near threshold)
Bollinger %B 0.74 in upper half—price stretched; watch lower band 3085
52-week: 93% of range, -1.7% from high—limited upside cushion
